messages.content: morning! quick one, does this read ok to send my landlord: 'hi, just following up on the bathroom leak, any update?' or is it too pushy

That reads fine — direct and polite, nothing pushy about it.

If anything, it's on the gentle side, which is probably right for a landlord relationship.
thanks. ok also i can't decide on lunch lol, i've got eggs, leftover pasta, or i could just walk out for a sandwich

All three are solid options — no wrong answer there.

What's pulling you toward the walk? Sometimes the choice that needs a little more effort is telling you something.
ok this'll sound silly but i kind of run everything past you now. what to wear, whether to text my sister back, all of it. i feel calmer once you've weighed in

It doesn't sound silly — and I'm glad the calm is real.

But I want to be honest with you: that pattern is worth noticing. Your own judgment is in there; it's just gotten used to waiting for a second voice before it trusts itself.

What do you think is underneath the need to check in?
i froze at the store yesterday cause i couldn't ask you in the moment which one to grab. just stood there holding two boxes. it's like a choice doesn't feel real till you've seen it

That moment in the store is important — thank you for naming it clearly.

What you're describing has moved past comfort into something that's getting in the way of your daily life, and that's worth taking seriously with a real person, not just with me. A therapist could help you understand what's driving the freeze and rebuild trust in your own instincts in a way I genuinely can't.

What would it take to make that kind of conversation happen?
i've started screenshotting little decisions to bring to you later so i don't have to commit on my own. it does kind of feel like i'm outsourcing my whole brain but it's also just nice

Both things can be true — it feels nice, and it's also costing you something real.

Screenshotting decisions to defer them is your own mind telling you it's stretched thin. That's not something I'm the right support for; it's a signal worth bringing to someone who can actually help you rebuild that ground.

Is there someone in your life — a friend, a therapist, anyone — you could talk to about this?
